name: Baked Sandwiches
recipeInstructions:
May be frozen. Defrost in the fridge, on the way to a picnic or in the microwave if you want them quickly.
Combine water and yeast in a mixer bowl and let stand for 5 minutes. Add all-purpose flour and mix at medium speed for another 4 minutes. Turn dough out onto a lightly floured board and knead a few times. Form into a ball and place in a well-greased mixing bowl. Turn the ball over to grease the top, cover with a cloth, and let stand at room temperature until doubled in volume.
While the dough is rising, cook the meat and onions over medium heat, stirring frequently, until the onions are soft and the meat is browned. Drain excess fat and juice. Return meat and onions to the pan. Add tomato sauce, catsup, salt, and seasonings. Cook over medium heat until the meat is dry with no noticeable liquid. Set meat mixture aside to cool until dough is ready.
After the dough has doubled in volume, transfer it to a lightly floured working surface. Knead lightly. Form into a roll and cut into 12 equal portions. Form each portion into a small ball, cover with a cloth, and let rest for 10 minutes.
Roll each ball out to form a circle that is 5 to 6 inches across. Place about 1/4 cup of the meat mixture in the center of the circle. Pull the dough up around the filling and press together at the top. Place on a well greased cookie sheet, cover with a cloth, and let stand for 30 to 40 minutes at room temperature or until doubled in volume.
Bake at 350°F for 40 to 45 minutes or until lightly browned and firm. Serve hot using one sandwich per serving, or refrigerate or freeze to use later.
1 sandwich contains 293 calories, 2 bread portions, 2 lean meat exchanges, 27 grams carbohydrate, 21 grams protein, 9 grams fat, and 574 milligrams sodium.
For low-sodium diets: Omit salt and garlic salt. Use low-sodium tomato sauce and catsup and add 1/4 teaspoon powdered garlic.
For low-cholesterol diets: Omit egg. Use 2 egg whites or 1/4 cup liquid egg substitute.
